rationale: Castano et al. (2008) is used in hypothesis framing about temporal distance effects on product evaluation, not just in a generic literature list. The citing paper explicitly returns to Castano et al. in the General Discussion, contrasts its own pattern with that prior result, and explains why results differ under cognitive versus affective processing modes. This shows the citation helps position and sharpen the citing paper's claimed contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Castano et al. (2008) as prior evidence on temporal distance and mental simulation, then qualifies that prior pattern by introducing processing mode as a boundary condition. It argues that Castano-type effects map to an affective pattern, while cognitive-mode results can reverse the effect.
